lines: Jeremiah keeps one hand at his throat, checking that the black cord is still there.
The lost purse is back on his belt. His entry papers stick out of the coat pocket he swore was empty.
"Now I believe my wife is going to laugh until winter."

choices.label: Ask whether he still plans to reach Hallowfen
Leave him with his recovered property

lines: Jeremiah has emptied both coat pockets onto his blanket. Washers and bent wire lie where money and papers should be.
He sees the tin saint in your pack and stops searching.
"That belongs around my neck. Priscilla sent you all this way?"

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: Priscilla’s necklace has reached Jeremiah.

lines: My papers were rolled inside the blanket, and my purse is still under the kettle.
So nobody robbed me. The freight clerk even found my Hallowfen seal.
Priscilla will never call this chance.

choices.label: Ask how he ended up here

lines: Jeremiah works a pump key through the seam of an empty purse, checking for a tear that is not there.
"I had Hallowfen work and entered-person papers. The gate clerk found the work order. The papers vanished. No proof I belonged north of the chain, so south I went."

choices.label: Leave him to search the seam again

lines: "At the gate I still had the work order. My entered-person papers were gone, and the twelve ducats went missing before South Measure wrote me onto Fire Seven."
"After that my boot split, the freight bell rang early, and Fire Seven gave me the one blanket under the roof leak. A man notices a pattern."
"I checked every seam twice. Someone cleaned me out without touching my buttons."
Jeremiah slips the cord over his head and works the knot flat with a mechanic’s thumbnail. The tin saint settles against his chest.
He presses an old brass road chit into your hand.
"Hallowfen called it history. Another gate may still respect it. You carried Priscilla’s worry farther than I carried my sense, so take it."

choices.label: Watch what the saint does next
